description: The noexec mount option can be used to prevent binaries from being executed out of /var. Add the noexec option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /var.
rationale:
The <tt>/var</tt> directory contains variable system data such as logs,
mails and caches. No binaries should be executed from this directory.
ocil_clause: the "/var" file system does not have the "noexec" option set
ocil:
Verify the noexec option is configured for the /var mount point, run the following command: 
$ sudo mount | grep '\s/var\s'
. . . /var . . . noexec . . .
title: Add noexec Option to /var
